papers.title: Allis (1994) — Searching for Solutions in Games and AI
papers.summary: Foundational text on solving games. Defines ultra-weak, weak, and strong solution strengths. Introduces proof-number search and estimates Awari's search space.

papers.title: Herik, Uiterwijk & van Rijswijck (2002) — Games Solved: Now and in the Future
papers.summary: Standard survey tabulating state-space and game-tree complexities for ~25 games including Awari. Best single overview of where Oware sits in the landscape of solved games.

papers.title: Broline & Loeb (1995) — Combinatorics of Mancala: Ayo, Tchoukaillon, and 1/π
papers.summary: Pure mathematics of sowing/capture endgames. Derives periodicity of winning patterns and proves seed count grows asymptotically like n²/π.

papers.title: Heule & Rothkrantz — Solving Games: Applicable Procedures
papers.summary: Maps which solving techniques (retrograde analysis, αβ, proof-number, databases) apply to which game properties. Awari is convergent — why retrograde analysis works.

papers.title: Set-Based Retrograde Analysis (2024)
papers.summary: Modern symbolic/BDD-style approach to retrograde analysis. Useful for computing or compressing endgame tablebases.

papers.title: CG 2000 Proceedings (LNCS 2063) — Computers and Games
papers.summary: Contains van der Goot's Awari Retrograde Analysis and van Rijswijck's Learning from Perfection — two key Oware papers.

papers.title: Lincke (2002) — Computational Limits of Large Exhaustive Search
papers.summary: Deep treatment of retrograde analysis with limited memory and the value representation used for Awari endgame databases. Primary source for the EDB design.

papers.title: Supervised vs. Unsupervised ML for an Awale/Mancala Player
papers.summary: Compares ML techniques for evolving an Awari-playing agent. Practical reference for a web-game-sized AI opponent.

papers.title: Daoud et al. (2004) — Ayo: Representation Trumps Search
papers.summary: Argues richer board representation beats raw search depth — relevant to whether evaluation features matter more than ply count.

papers.title: Irving, Donkers & Uiterwijk (2000) — Solving Kalah
papers.summary: Sister result on Kalah variant. Uses iterative-deepening MTD(f), move ordering, TT, futility pruning, and endgame databases.

papers.title: ACG 2023 — Solution Methods for Games
papers.summary: Recent survey of algorithmic approaches to solving combinatorial games, covering modern advances in retrograde analysis and forward search.

reports.title: Synthesis: Engine Roadmap
reports.summary: High-level roadmap synthesizing all research findings into the oware-web engine development plan.

reports.title: Defense: Architecture Decisions
reports.summary: Rationale for major architecture decisions in oware-web, grounded in the research findings.

reports.title: Report 01 — Foundational Papers
reports.summary: Close reading of the Maastricht school papers that define how the field talks about solving games.

reports.title: Report 02 — Endgame Database Spec
reports.summary: Implementation spec for a perfect-play endgame database via retrograde analysis.

reports.title: Report 03 — Combinatorics (Broline & Loeb)
reports.summary: The pure mathematics of the Oware endgame: sowing patterns, periodicity, and 1/π.

reports.title: Report 04 — Solving Procedures (Heule & Rothkrantz)
reports.summary: Which solving procedures Oware actually needs, based on its convergent game properties.

reports.title: Report 05 — ML Player (Randle et al.)
reports.summary: Can we get a strong AI without a giant tablebase? Supervised vs unsupervised ML comparison.

reports.title: Report 06 — Lincke's Endgame Engineering
reports.summary: Deep dive into Lincke's Awari retrograde analysis and cycle handling.

reports.title: Report 07 — van der Goot's Forward-Move Retrograde
reports.summary: The algorithm our EDB builder uses, validated against van der Goot's original.

reports.title: Report 08 — van Rijswijck: Learning from Perfection
reports.summary: Mining perfect endgame databases to learn evaluation functions without shipping a 178 GB tablebase.

reports.title: Report 09 — Daoud: Representation vs Search
reports.summary: How Ayo's richer representation architecture outperforms deeper search.

reports.title: Report 10 — Irving: Solving Kalah
reports.summary: Lessons from solving the Kalah variant: MTD(f), enhanced TT cut-offs, and endgame databases.

reports.title: Report 11 — Fang: Chinese Chess Endgame Databases
reports.summary: Cross-domain endgame database engineering lessons applicable to Oware's EDB.

overview.title: Mathematical Architecture & Pure-Game Mathematics
overview.summary: The mathematics realized in the program, the broader pure mathematics of Oware, and gaps between the two.

overview.title: Architecture Decisions — Engine v1
overview.summary: Key design choices for the v1 engine build: single-file approach, Worker architecture, EDB format, and evaluation strategy.

overview.title: Game Evolution Notes
overview.summary: Design journal tracking the evolution of gameplay features, rule variants, and UI decisions.

bibliography.title: Oware / Awari — Research Bibliography
bibliography.summary: Full bibliography for the project. Downloaded PDFs live in ./papers/. Gated items list stable links.
